title: Home Infra Protocol Status Snapshot
description: Sanitized runtime telemetry output published by a project-owned Telemetry Source. The snapshot records what the producer observed; consumers derive freshness by joining observed_at with the job declaration's stale_after.

observed_at.description: UTC RFC3339 timestamp for when the producer observed or completed the check. Producers MUST write this; consumers derive freshness from it.

next_run_at.description: Optional UTC RFC3339 timestamp for the next execution planned by the producer's scheduler. Consumers may render it as an attributed countdown but MUST NOT derive lateness, freshness, or incidents from it. Producers omit it when no authoritative next execution is known.

summary.description: Display-only human summary. It SHOULD be a plain-language operator statement rather than an internal field name or key=value expression. Consumers MUST NOT parse this field for logic.

checks.description: Optional shaped sub-checks, for example one check per account or upstream. The top-level snapshot remains the producer's aggregate view.

top_level_condition.description: Producer-emitted aggregate condition. Producers emit only ok or degraded; consumers derive unknown/down from absence, unreadability, or staleness.

check_condition.description: Sub-check condition. Unlike the top-level producer condition, a producer may mark an individual sub-check down while the aggregate remains degraded.

severity.description: Producer-recommended ordered severity: none=0, info=1, watch=2, warning=3, critical=4. Consumers apply policy on top of this recommendation.

name.description: Stable machine-readable check identifier. Consumers MUST use this field for joins and logic and SHOULD prefer label for display when label is present.

label.description: Optional concise human-facing check label. Producers SHOULD provide it when the stable name would expose implementation syntax or jargon in an operator UI.

summary.description: Display-only human summary for the sub-check. It SHOULD be a plain-language statement rather than an internal field name or key=value expression. Consumers MUST NOT parse this field for logic.
